title: pelargonium mutans
intro: Pelargonium mutans, also known as Mountain Rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. It is a perennial herb native to South Africa, where it is traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as geraniol, linalool, and citronellal, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, providing health benefits such as reduced fever, relief from respiratory issues, and improved digestion. In herbal preparations, Pelargonium mutans is often used as an infusion, tincture, or syrup, and is commonly used in teas, cough syrups, and topical creams to soothe and calm various health conditions.
